title: Do not default to swipeable onboarding carousels
summary: AI-generated onboarding flows universally use a 3-4 screen horizontal swipeable carousel with full-width illustration, headline, description, dot indicators, and 'Next'/'Get Started' navigation.
instruction: Replace linear swipeable onboarding with progressive disclosure that teaches the product through actual use. Consider preference-setting screens, contextual tips, or interactive tutorials over the carousel format.
rationale: The swipeable carousel onboarding trains users to skip content. Users swipe through to reach the app, absorbing little to nothing. The illustration-on-top format prioritizes decoration over information, and the fixed slide count forces content into an unnatural narrative structure.
violations: 3-4 horizontal swipeable screens with dot indicators
Full-width illustration at top of each screen
'Welcome' or 'Introducing X' as the first screen
'Next' button on intermediate screens, 'Get Started' on final
Abstract illustrations that do not show actual product UI
controls: Interactive onboarding where users configure preferences as they proceed
Contextual tooltips that reveal features when first encountered
Blank-slate experience that guides the first action
Progressive disclosure: reveal features gradually as user competence grows
borderlineCases: Consumer apps where the core value proposition needs narrative explanation
contextualExceptions: Regulated apps that must present specific disclosures before first use
detectorSignal: A horizontal scrollable container with 3-4 full-height child screens, each containing an illustration at top and text below, with dot indicators and a 'Next'/'Get Started' button sequence.
matcherProposal: Detect a multi-screen onboarding flow with horizontal swipe behavior, dot position indicators, and sequential navigation buttons (Next → Get Started).
detectorLimitations: Cannot assess if the carousel format is appropriate for the product
falsePositiveScenarios: Products with genuinely sequential concepts to explain
mitigationStrategy: Flag as a suggestion to evaluate whether the carousel onboarding is the most effective approach or if interactive onboarding would serve users better.

fixturePlans.violation: 4-screen onboarding: Welcome illustration, Feature 1, Feature 2, Get Started. Dot indicators, Next/Get Started buttons.
fixturePlans.control: Preference setup onboarding: pick interests, set preferences, see personalized content immediately.
fixturePlans.borderline: Banking app with 3 regulatory disclosure screens that must be viewed sequentially.

cleanRoomAttestations: Developed without access to proprietary prompt collections.
